summary: Exhaustive frontend link/anchor testing for Riya Singh static portfolio SPA after the `section[id] { scroll-margin-top: 88px }` CSS fix. ALL 7 top-nav anchor links, both hero CTAs, nav logo, desktop+mobile resume download, all Connect section links (email/linkedin/github/send-email/resume), Community Instagram event link, Projects card 1 (WDC research) Document + Published links, placeholder no-link cards (0/2/3), and Cert filter buttons were verified end-to-end on both desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x800) viewports. The CSS scroll-margin-top fix is confirmed working: every section heading sits at ~88px below the top after anchor click, no longer hidden behind the fixed glass nav.

community_event_link.aria_label: View the event post on Instagram — opens in a new tab

critical_code_review_comments: Everything works as designed. Minor (non-blocking) observations: (1) `connect-email`, `connect-send-email-btn`, and `connect-resume-btn` do not have `target=_blank` — this is correct (mailto and same-origin download should not open new tabs). (2) The `download` attribute on resume buttons resolves to an empty string in DOM (`download=""`) — this is standard HTML and means the browser will use the default filename; behavior is correct. (3) `scroll-margin-top: 88px` was applied globally to all `section[id]` which is the right call given the fixed nav height of ~80–88px. No issues.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All link-related functionality verified working post scroll-margin-top fix. User's 'links not working' complaint is resolved — anchors now scroll so the section heading is visible below the fixed glass nav. Resume PDF reachable at /resume.pdf (200, application/pdf). No broken href='#' anywhere in rendered DOM (placeholder projects correctly render the 'Links coming soon' span instead). If new projects are added later with real github/demo URLs, ensure linkMeta() in Projects.jsx still renders them. Community Instagram link is live. Cert section remains in empty-state until CERTIFICATES array in content.js is populated.
rca of the issue: User reported 'links not working'. Root cause: top of each section was being scrolled to y=0 of viewport, which sat *behind* the fixed glass nav (~80–88px tall), making the section-label and h2 invisible — giving the impression that anchors did nothing. Mitigation applied by E1: added `section[id] { scroll-margin-top: 88px }` to /app/frontend/src/index.css combined with the existing `html { scroll-behavior: smooth }`. Verified: every section's top after an anchor click is ~88px (i.e. flush below nav, heading visible). Issue fully resolved.
